ogTitle: Cape Paterson vs Cape Schanck: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cape Paterson and Cape Schanck.
heading: Cape Paterson vs Cape Schanck — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cape Paterson, Victoria and Cape Schanck, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cape Paterson has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Cape Schanck (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cape Paterson or Cape Schanck safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cape Paterson has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Cape Schanck scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cape Paterson scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Cape Schanck scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cape Paterson or Cape Schanck?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cape Paterson has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
